key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: Marvin filings: FY26 guided EBITDA midpoint × 18% FCF conversion / shares (haircut vs 25% company target); proof-first bridge 2026-07-21.
Marvin filings primary — independent of McIntyre normalization

open_questions: Confirm POC divestiture with company 8-K or IR statement
Reconcile segment EBITDA allocation to your own model
Size: watch vs small accumulate if sale confirmed
thesis: QuidelOrtho is a post-merger diagnostics company (Quidel plus Ortho Clinical Diagnostics, 2022). **[Fact]** It sells lab instruments, transfusion-medicine tests, point-of-care rapid assays, and early-stage molecular diagnostics to hospitals, labs, and blood centers. **[Fact]** Revenue is largely recurring consumables on installed bases. **[Fact]** FY2025 revenue was **$2,730.2M** (-1.9% YoY per `10-K_20260219...htm`); adjusted EBITDA was **$597.0M** (21.9% margin). Q1 FY2026 revenue was **$619.8M** (-10.5% YoY per `10-Q_20260506...htm`). FY2026 guidance: revenue **$2.70 to $2.75B**; adjusted EBITDA **$615 to $630M** (company deck).
why_market_wrong: The stock near **~$13.79** prices consolidated distress at roughly **5.4 times** enterprise value to EBITDA, while **Labs plus Immunohematology** (75% of revenue) grew mid-single digits ex-COVID in FY2025. **[Inference]** Margin expansion (+240 bps FY2025) is real, but revenue still falls and leverage (~4 times net debt to EBITDA) caps the multiple. **[Fact]** On **June 27, 2026**, the Financial Times reported QuidelOrtho is exploring a **~$1.5 billion** sale of its point-of-care unit (Advent International among interested buyers) to cut merger debt. **[Inference]** If completed, that is a dated deleveraging catalyst not in our base Lawrence IRR until SEC confirmation.
